questions.prompt: What is the typical problem with turning using yaw only (just turning the nose)?
options: The drone climbs very quickly
The turn is very tight but slow and 'sloppy'; the drone slides sideways
The drone doesn't turn at all
The propellers spin in reverse
questions.explanation: Yaw alone rotates the drone almost in place; it gives a tight but slow turn that sideslips. That's why it's combined with roll.

questions.prompt: What does turning with roll (banking) make the drone do?
options: Banks the drone into the direction of the turn and, with forward speed, carves a wide arc (like an airplane)
Only turns the nose without changing position
Lifts the drone straight up vertically
Stops the motors
questions.explanation: Roll banks the drone into the direction you want to turn; with forward speed, this carves the drone into a wide arc, just like an airplane banking into a turn.

questions.prompt: What does a coordinated turn mean?
options: Turning by applying throttle only
Banking with roll to start the turn and using yaw to align the nose with the turn direction to prevent sideslip
Stopping the propellers one by one
Keeping the drone perfectly level and sliding it sideways
questions.explanation: In a coordinated turn, roll starts the turn (sets the radius) and yaw aligns the nose with the direction of travel; so the drone carves a clean circle with its nose on the path, without sliding sideways.

questions.prompt: Why does the drone lose altitude when it banks into a turn?
options: Because the battery weakens
Because the propellers slow down
Because part of the thrust now goes sideways, and the remaining upward part isn't enough to overcome gravity
Because air resistance disappears
questions.explanation: In a hover, thrust is entirely upward. When the drone banks, part of the thrust points sideways; the upward component decreases, and when it's no longer enough to overcome gravity, the drone descends.

questions.prompt: What do you need to do in a turn to carve a smooth, constant-altitude circle?
options: Cut the throttle completely
Add a little throttle during the turn to compensate for the altitude loss caused by banking
Use yaw only and never use roll
Move the sticks as hard and abruptly as possible
questions.explanation: Because the bank angle causes altitude loss, you add a little throttle as you enter the turn and ease it back as you level out. This keeps the circle at a constant altitude.
